name: Las Zetas de la Pedriza y Manzanares el Real — Sierra de Guadarrama
region_intro: Mountain biking around El Boalo offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, making it a prime destination for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by the unique granite formations of La Pedriza, featuring rocky cliffs and numerous peaks. Riders can explore varied ecosystems, including oak and pine forests, alongside ash groves and riparian copses. This landscape provides a range of natural features suitable for different mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around El Boalo The most popular mountain bike trail is Las Zetas de la Pedriza y Manzanares el Real — Sierra de Guadarrama, a 36.7 miles (59.1 km) trail that takes 6 hours 6 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the significant granite range of La Pedriza. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Views of La Pedriza – La Pedriza Corta loop from El Boalo, a difficult 23.2 miles (37.3 km) path. This route offers expansive views of the La Pedriza geological feature. Local mountain bikers also love the Pine of the Chain – Chiquillo Spring and Reservoir loop from El Berrocal, a 25.6 miles (41.2 km) trail leading through pine forests and past natural springs, often completed in about 3 hours 58 minutes. Mountain biking around El Boalo is defined by the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain range, the granite formations of La Pedriza, and diverse forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in El Boalo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Boalo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around El Boalo?
A: El Boalo offers a wide variety of mountain bike trails, with over 45 routes available. These trails cater to all skill levels, including 22 easy, 10 moderate, and 15 difficult options, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in El Boalo?
A: Mountain biking in El Boalo features diverse terrain within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. You'll encounter the unique granite formations of La Pedriza, characterized by rocky cliffs and numerous peaks. Routes also traverse varied ecosystems, including oak and pine forests, ash groves, and riparian copses, offering a dynamic and engaging experience.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in El Boalo?
A: Yes, El Boalo has several easy mountain bike trails. A good option for beginners or families is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23944516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir – Santillana Reservoir Bridge loop from El Boalo-Cerceda-Mataelpino&lt;/a&gt;. This 21.6 km route offers a more gentle ride around the reservoir.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore the majestic granite formations of La Pedriza, the highest peak of Peñalara with its glacial cirque, and diverse forests. Along the trails, you might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/641391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Road of Puerto de la Fuenfría&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2555399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3692534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalara Glacial Cirque&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that offer great views of La Pedriza?
A: Absolutely. For expansive views of the La Pedriza geological feature, consider the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34395903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of La Pedriza – La Pedriza Corta loop from El Boalo&lt;/a&gt;. This 37.3 km route is a top favorite among local mountain bikers for its scenic panoramas.

Q: Can I find any waterfalls near the mountain bike trails in El Boalo?
A: Yes, the area around El Boalo features several beautiful waterfalls. You might discover highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2877011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covacho Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5713612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ducha de los Alemanes&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3526520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Socrates Pool&lt;/a&gt;, which add to the scenic diversity of the region.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in El Boalo?
A: The mountain bike trails in El Boalo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in El Boalo?
A: Many of the mountain bike routes in El Boalo are designed as loops, offering circular journeys. An example is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34413311" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pine of the Chain – Chiquillo Spring and Reservoir loop from El Berrocal&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through pine forests and past natural springs.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in El Boalo?
A: El Boalo's diverse ecosystems and mountain climate mean that spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow to higher peaks like Peñalara, offering different challenges and experiences.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers?
A: Experienced mountain bikers will find plenty of challenging routes. The difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1348735288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Zetas de la Pedriza y Manzanares el Real — Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/a&gt; is a 59.1 km trail that traverses the significant granite range of La Pedriza, offering a demanding ride with considerable elevation changes.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I see while mountain biking in El Boalo?
A: The diverse ecosystems of the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park support a rich variety of flora and fauna. While mountain biking, you might spot mountain goats, various bird species like Griffon Vultures, and if you're lucky, even the Spanish Imperial Eagle, especially in the rocky outcrops and pine forests.

The Collado de los Pastores is a beautiful landmark in the Sierra de Guadarrama, located in the area of La Pedriza, near Manzanares el Real, in the Community of Madrid. This hill offers stunning panoramic views of the region, including the Bola del Mundo, the Cuerda Larga and the granite Pedriza.
One of the popular routes to reach the Collado de los Pastores begins at the Canto Cochino car park. From there, you ascend the Cuerda de los Porrones, passing by the Mirador de la Pedriza, until you reach the hill.
Be careful with the descent, it can be dangerous because the bike picks up a lot of speed
Spectacular viewpoint overlooking the entire Pedriza and half of Madrid. The final climb is tough, but worth it
After the zetas, a good place to regain strength
One of the attractions of the route is to enjoy the streams of the rivers that run throughout the park. A particularly refreshing sound in Summer

On the right bank of the Manzanares River, the Old Castle of Manzanares El Real stands on a hill, guarding La Pedriza, the Cañada Real Segoviana and the river itself. The fortress, whose shape and exterior walls are preserved, was the first castle inhabited by the Mendoza family and which served as a defense against Moors and Christians.

The history and image of Manzanares El Real will always be linked to the Mendoza family and their unmistakable castle, one of the jewels, not only of Madrid's military architecture, but of all the medieval architecture of the kingdom of Castile.

The Plaza del Pueblo in Manzanares el Real is the heart of this charming town in the Community of Madrid. This square is a meeting and celebration place for residents and visitors alike, and is surrounded by historic and picturesque buildings.
The route between the reservoir and the Batán bridge is recommended

Nice views of the reservoir.
Usual transit area for Madrid - Segovia and many other routes.
More at https://bigjoe.es/reto-superado-madrid-segovia-mtb-completada/
Enjoy the views, the mountains and the Castle of Manzanares in the background, the Santillana reservoir at your feet.
